item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: CONTROL Resonant launches September 24
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Supernatural action RPG dated.

Supernatural action RPG CONTROL Resonant will launch for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam and Epic Games Store on September 24, developer Remedy Entertainment announced.

Pre-orders are available now in the following editions:

  • Physical
    • Standard Edition (PS5, Xbox Series) ($59.99 / £49.99 / €59.99)
      • A copy of the game
    • SteelBook Edition ($69.99 / £59.99 / €69.99)
      • A copy of the game
      • SteelBook Case
      • Fine art prints (x3)
      • Key art poster
  • Digital
    • Standard Edition (PS5, Xbox Series, PC) ($59.99 / £49.99 / €59.99)
      • A copy of the game
    • Digital Deluxe Edition (Xbox Series, PC) ($69.99 / £59.99 / €69.99)
      • A copy of the game
      • AWE Mission Outfit for Dylan
      • Exclusive Untapped Artifact (Wallet)
      • Starter Resource Bundle
      • Digital art book
      • Original soundtrack
    • Digital Deluxe Edition (PlayStation Exclusive) (PS5) ($69.99 / £59.99 / €69.99)
      • A copy of the game
      • 48 hours of early access
      • AWE Mission Outfit for Dylan
      • Exclusive Untapped Artifact (Wallet)
      • Starter Resource Bundle
      • Digital art book
      • Original soundtrack

“As he navigates a distorted Manhattan shaped by paranatural forces and shifting realities, the story explores themes of identity, power, and sacrifice,” CONTOL RESONANT creative director Mikael Kasurinen said in a press release. “Once a test subject of the FBC, Dylan must now harness his evolving abilities, confront his past, and learn to trust others in a world he was never meant to be part of. In doing so, he must rediscover his humanity—and decide what he’s willing to lose to save what remains.”

Remedy Entertainment chief commercial officer and head of publishing Johannes Paloheimo added, “CONTROL Resonant is Remedy Entertainment’s most expansive game ever, complete with incredible gameplay, narrative twists and challenging creativity that will keep old fans and new engaged and entertained like nothing we have created before. As Remedy is self-publishing CONTROL Resonant, our global marketing efforts will be just as ambitious in its execution and style, evidenced by this inclusion in the State of Play and leading us into Summer Game Fest and beyond. We will have a number of events and opportunities for fans over the summer as we move towards the game’s release on September 24.”

  • ❮
  • ❯

Here are the latest details, via Remedy Entertainment:

The CONTROL Resonant Story trailer explores Dylan Faden’s (played by Sean Durrie) dramatic journey as he is sent by the Federal Bureau of Control (FBC) into the heart of a supernatural crisis. A new, otherworldly form of resonance, alongside the Hiss and the Mold, is warping the city as a mysterious cosmic force reshapes reality itself. For this mission, Dylan is joined by a new FBC ally Zoe de Vera, alongside returning fan favorites Simon Arish and Emily Pope from the original Control. With his sister, Federal Bureau of Control Director Jesse Faden, missing, Dylan must step into a role he was never prepared for—humanity’s last line of defense.

As part of Remedy’s efforts to bring CONTROL Resonant worldwide, the game will be the most localized Remedy game to date. CONTROL Resonant will feature full audio, interface, and subtitles in English, Brazilian Portuguese, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, and Spanish (Spain). Additional interface and subtitle support will be available in Korean, Polish, Russian, Spanish (Latin America), Traditional Chinese, Turkish, and Ukrainian.

CONTROL Resonant will also launch with support for GeForce NOW. The Mac version via Steam and the App Store will follow later this year.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Story Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/control-resonant-launches-september-24
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: KEMURI launches in 2027 for PS5
item.content:encoded:
• PS5

First look at UNSEEN's yokai possession action game.

KEMURI, the 2023-anniounced yokai possession action game from Ikumi Nakamura-led studio UNSEEN, will launch for PlayStation 5 in 2027, the developer announced.

Here is an overview of the game, via UNSEEN:

KEMURI is a yokai possession action game where you make contracts with yokai, cloak yourself in their power, and fight. The stage is Kemuri City—“the city closest to the afterlife.” Explore this vertical city, where the boundary between the living world and the afterlife blurs, and face unknown beings as a yokai hunter. Defeating yokai is just the beginning—by contracting with them, allowing possession, and wielding their power, everything changes: your appearance, your fighting style, everything. Supports solo or online cooperative play for up to three players.

Watch a new trailer and explainer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Gameplay Reveal Trailer

What is KEMURI? Ikumi Nakamura Explains

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/kemuri-launches-in-2027-for-ps5
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: No Rest for the Wicked launches in October for PS5 and PC, later for Xbox Series and Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

Action RPG currently in Early Access.

Action RPG No Rest for the Wicked will leave Early Access and launch in version 1.0 for PlayStation 5 and PC via Steam in October, developer Moon Studios announced. Xbox Series and Switch 2 versions will follow at a later date.

No Rest for the Wicked first launched in Early Access for PC on April 18, 2024. The Early Access version is currently available at a limited-time 30 percent-off discount price in commemoration of today’s announcement.

Here is an overview of the game, via Moon Studios:

As a token of appreciation for supporters new and old, Moon Studios is offering an exclusive Early Access Founder’s Pack, a free limited edition bundle available for anyone who purchases the game on PC prior to July 10, 2026, with rewards unlocking when 1.0 officially launches. PlayStation 5 users can receive the Early Access Founder’s Pack by pre-ordering the game once available, any time before launch day.

  • A New Secret Hideout: Spirit Cavern – The legacy of Moon Studios now lives inside No Rest for the Wicked, exclusively for Founders. A hand-crafted home that bridges both worlds.
  • Sayer’s Vow – An all new Sword in the stone reserved just for the founders that only early adopters can extract in the game world.
  • A Custom Founders Tag – Ensures players are clearly identified as early adopters.
  • As a Founder member, players will get access to an exclusive Public Beta Test Realm reserved just for them, even after the 1.0 game launch.
  • ❮
  • ❯

The full 1.0 release will be a massive expansion of the core No Rest for the Wicked experience, offering a vastly expanded breadth of content for both solo players and multiplayer lovers. It introduces a staggering over 60 hours of new content, a new horde mode, powerful new weapons, bosses and enemies, new areas and a completely reimagined class system, culminating in one of the most expansive soulslike action-RPGs to date.

  • Sprawling New Areas – Explore the full game’s 15+ hand-crafted regions filled with secrets, terrifying bosses, and intricate pathways.
  • Increased Gameplay Flexibility and Deep Progression – An all-new class and progression system allows players to fine-tune their Cerim warrior to their exact style. Two players using the exact same weapon will play nothing alike.
  • Persistent Multiplayer – Players can share their journey in free private realms where the world evolves even when the realm’s original creator is offline.
  • Final Story Arc – Politics. Revenge. Passion. The ending players have been theorizing about since 2024, including tons of hand crafted cinematic scenes will be part of 1.0 and it is worth every hour players have put into the Sacra.
  • Endgame Content and Replayability – Players will test their skills in challenging new endgame activities designed for the most dedicated warriors.
  • Cross-Play and Cross-Save – Full cross-play and cross-save between PC and console at launch.

No REST for the Wicked is the highly celebrated action RPG from Moon Studios, the same team behind the critically acclaimed Ori and the Blind Forest and Ori and the Will of the Wisps. The game is an enticing blend of dark storytelling, painterly visuals, and best-in-class Soulslike combat and RPG design.

Players take on the role of a “Cerim,” a member of a holy order imbued with mysterious powers, tasked with destroying the Pestilence, an ancient plague that has begun to consume the island Isola Sacra, itself already the site of great political upheaval and death. Players can collect and craft weapons, items, and more to turn the tide of each battle, upgrade their character and personal homestead, and play their part in an epic tale of bloodshed and bravery.

Since its launch in Early Access, No Rest for the Wicked has been supported with multiple major updates, including cooperative multiplayer, player-versus-player duels, and expanded combat possibilities.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/no-rest-for-the-wicked-launches-in-october-for-ps5-and-pc-later-for-xbox-series-and-switch-2
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Phantom Blade Zero delayed to October 29
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone,

As many of you just saw in today’s State of Play, pre-orders for Phantom Blade Zero will open this summer!

At the same time, I want to share another update: Phantom Blade Zero will now launch on October 29, 2026, moved from its previous date of September 9, 2026.

First of all, I want to offer my sincere apologies to all the players who have been following and supporting us. This was not an easy decision. More than anyone, we understand the expectations our players have placed on us. And precisely because of those expectations, we do not want to release Phantom Blade Zero knowing there is still an opportunity to take it one step further.

Looking back over the past few years, the development of Phantom Blade Zero has been a thrilling journey of evolution. As we built the game, we continued learning, refining our technology, and expanding the scale and resources behind the project. From its first reveal in 2023, to its first offline hands-on demo in 2024, to the Seven-Star Sword Formation, Lion Dance, and Drunken Sword showcased in 2025, Phantom Blade Zero has continued to evolve year after year, with each new showing representing a clear leap forward from the last. And we have shared that evolution with players around the world every step of the way.

Over the past few months, we have been streamlining content and focusing our efforts on polishing the areas that matter most. During this process, I saw one final opportunity for Phantom Blade Zero to make another meaningful leap forward.

We have upgraded a number of character models and reworked many environments across the game, pushing them toward the highest standard we can currently achieve. We have also spent additional effort preserving as much of this visual impact as possible even without relying on ray tracing. Of course, ray tracing will further enhance the visuals, but our priority is to make sure the core look, atmosphere, and intensity of Phantom Blade Zero come through at full force for as many players as possible.

A 50-day delay cannot solve everything. But it does give us enough time to complete a number of clearly defined and genuinely important improvements. These refinements will directly affect how the game feels when players first step into the world of Phantom Blade Zero. We could have delivered some of them through post-launch updates, but for the players who choose to join us on day one, we believe they deserve the best version of Phantom Blade Zero we can deliver from the very beginning.

I am truly sorry for the delay, and I want to thank everyone who has continued to follow and support Phantom Blade Zero. This summer, we will have much more to show you. I believe every extra day of waiting will be worth it.

I’d also like to share what you can expect next:

  1. A new trailer that will release alongside pre-orders, featuring entirely new in-game footage.
  2. After pre-order opens, we will also have a dedicated State of Play focused entirely on Phantom Blade Zero: a 15-to-20-minute deep dive into the game’s world, combat, exploration, and character progression systems. The vast majority of what we show there will be brand-new.

We will share the exact dates in upcoming announcements!

Once again, thank you all for your continued support!

Soulframe
S-GAME CEO / Creator of Phantom Blade Zero

Watch a new trailer below.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/phantom-blade-zero-delayed-to-october-29
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
